svg.title: The fit policy decision record: fixed 1.4x with the boundary box and preview decided; overflow mark, fit-to-bounds, and width-fit plus scroll declined; auto-cycling deferred
svg.desc: Four option panels. Option A, fixed 1.4x plus the editor boundary box plus the wall preview, carries a decided badge: the type guarantee and determinism hold and the wall render stays untouched. Option B, a wall-side overflow mark, declined as standing chrome on a zero-interaction ambient surface. Option C, fit-to-bounds with a clamp, declined because type shrinks as layouts grow and editing changes the wall's scale. Option D, width-fit plus vertical scroll, surfaced by Xavier and declined because it re-opens the kiosk zero-interaction rule and re-enters the AOD-79 Fire OS touch bug. A strip defers auto-cycling pages as a named seam. A decision band records Xavier's call on 2026-07-03.
svg.text: The fit policy: the AOD-39 re-open, recorded
Five options surfaced with a recommendation before the doc froze (the AOD-71 / AOD-73 pattern); decided by Xavier, 2026-07-03.
DECIDED
A · FIXED 1.4x + BOX + PREVIEW
The type guarantee (across-the-room legibility) and
determinism hold; the wall render stays UNTOUCHED.
The box (§5) + the preview (§6) fix the authoring
blindness where the failure actually happened.
overflow = DEFINED clip, shown while arranging · the wall never warns
B · WALL-SIDE OVERFLOW MARK • declined
Fixed 1.4x + an edge fade whenever cards sit beyond
the fold. Honest on-wall, but it is STANDING CHROME
on an ambient surface kiosk-mode §5 makes zero-
interaction: never actionable where it appears.
the wall never nags; the editor is where the information belongs
C · FIT-TO-BOUNDS (CLAMPED) • declined
scale = clamp(fit(bbox), floor..1.4): layouts adapt, but
type SHRINKS as the wall gets busy (the guarantee dies
exactly when readability matters) and editing changes
the wall's scale (determinism dies).
clips past the clamp · "the cards, only larger" stops being true
D · WIDTH-FIT + VERTICAL SCROLL • declined
Surfaced by Xavier, engaged, declined: re-opens
kiosk-mode §5 (a scrolled-away wall holds its position;
the glance stops being trustworthy) AND re-enters the
AOD-79 Fire OS touch bug (wall content takes no touch
precisely so the exit corner works).
corner scrolls fight the hold-to-exit · type varies with width
E · AUTO-CYCLING PAGES • DEFERRED (THE NAMED SEAM, §11)
The zero-interaction way to show everything: viewport-sized pages on a slow dwell (the signage pattern).
Opened only on dogfood evidence that one frame is too small; pages = exact §3 window multiples.

text.tspan: Decision (Xavier, 2026-07-03): keep the fixed 1.4x; reaffirm AOD-39; fix authoring (the box + the preview).

svg.text: The build must not touch the wall render path.
kept for the record: "why 1.4x" = read at ~40cm in hand vs 2-3m on the wall · "why not arrange at 1.4x" = the editor is the workbench:
at 1x the whole frame fits with workspace around it; zoomed in you would see less than the frame; the preview is the honest wall-scale check
